Requirement: Claims must be submittable via a web-based digital form available 24/7
Rationale: Current paper process limits submission to office hours; digital intake removes this constraint and reduces handling lag

Requirement: The digital form must validate required fields before submission and prevent incomplete claims being entered into the system
Rationale: 18% rework rate is driven primarily by incomplete or inconsistent information at the point of entry

Requirement: Users must be able to upload supporting documents (photos
Rationale: PDFs) directly from the digital form up to 20MB per file
MoSCoW Priority: Field visits and photos are required evidence for many claim types; current process requires email follow-up for each document

Requirement: The system must generate a unique claim reference number immediately on submission and display it to the claimant
Rationale: Claimants currently have no reference and must call to track status — generating 35% of inbound support calls

Requirement: Claimants must be able to check claim status online using their claim reference number without calling
Rationale: 35% of inbound calls are status enquiries; self-service tracking removes this burden from the team entirely

Requirement: The system must send automated status update notifications to claimants at each stage of the process
Rationale: Lack of proactive communication is the top complaint in customer satisfaction data

Requirement: Claims must be automatically routed to the correct handler based on claim type and value within 1 hour of submission
Rationale: Manual routing is the primary cause of the processing delay — claims sit unassigned in a shared inbox for an average of 2 days
Notes: Biggest time saving in the workflow

Requirement: The system must provide handlers with a structured digital workflow that guides them through each step of the claims process
Rationale: Handlers currently rely on 4 unofficial Excel spreadsheets to compensate for the legacy system — this suggests the process guidance is inadequate
Notes: Excel workarounds are the strongest evidence

Requirement: The system must integrate with the existing finance system (Sage) to receive approved claim payments without manual re-entry
Rationale: Manual re-entry of payment data is the source of both errors and the 40% staff time overhead on data re-entry

Requirement: The system must support a manager approval workflow for claims above £5
Rationale: 000 before payment is processed
MoSCoW Priority: Regulatory requirement and existing internal control policy — must be maintained in the digital system

Requirement: A management dashboard must show live claims volumes
Rationale: processing times
Source: Management currently have no real-time visibility — they run weekly reports from the legacy system with a 7-day lag

Requirement: The dashboard must flag any claim that has exceeded the 5-day SLA target and is at risk of breaching
Rationale: SLA management is manual and reactive — managers find out after the breach

Requirement: The system must generate a monthly performance report showing claim volumes
Rationale: average processing time
Source: Required for board reporting and Benefits Realisation tracking against the approved business case KPIs

Requirement: The system must support role-based access: Claimant / Claims Handler / Senior Handler / Manager / Finance / Admin
Rationale: Current system has no role separation — all staff access the same screens regardless of function

Requirement: Staff accounts must be provisionable and deactivatable within 1 business day of an HR notification
Rationale: Staff turnover is 22% per year — current process for removing system access takes up to 2 weeks

Requirement: The system must support templated email communications to claimants at key process stages
Rationale: Handlers currently write individual emails at each stage — inconsistent and time-consuming

Requirement: Claimants must be able to securely message their handler through the portal rather than by phone or open email
Rationale: Phone is inefficient and unrecorded; open email creates data handling risks under GDPR

Requirement: Claimants must be able to submit claims and check status on a mobile device
Rationale: Over 60% of InsureRight web traffic comes from mobile devices — a desktop-only form would exclude the majority of claimants

Requirement: The system should integrate with the existing identity verification service (Experian) to verify claimant identity at point of submission
Rationale: Identity fraud is a known risk in the claims process; integration automates a manual check currently done by handlers

Requirement: The system should provide a REST API for future integration with third-party loss adjusters and repair partners
Rationale: InsureRight plans to onboard 3 new third-party partners in Year 2 — an API-first approach avoids point-to-point integrations

Requirement: Advanced analytics and trend analysis on claims data with predictive flagging of fraud patterns
Rationale: Fraud detection is valuable but not core to the immediate transformation; should be a future phase

Requirement: A native mobile app (iOS and Android) for claims submission and tracking
Rationale: A mobile-responsive web app will meet 80% of the mobile use case; native apps add cost for marginal benefit in Phase 1
Notes: Phase 2 if web app proves insufficient

Requirement: Live chat support within the portal during business hours
Rationale: Useful for claimant experience but staffing and tool cost are significant; evaluate after go-live

Requirement: Integration with a third-party vehicle data API to auto-populate vehicle details for motor claims
Rationale: Time-saving for motor claims specifically but affects only one claim type; not a priority for the core platform
